human_verification.notes: Validated production-accounting-specific boundaries, official production-paperwork and incentive sources, mismatch and override handling, validated packaging, and explicit specification-based runtime posture.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to production-accounting intake, posting support, payroll and fringe evidence review, petty-cash and AP support, bank and cost-report reconciliation, and incentive-support handling rather than generic accounting close or payroll administration.

Authority sources anchor the pack in California Film Commission guidelines and AUP materials, Georgia DOR audit materials, SAG-AFTRA signatory guidance, DGA agreements and rate cards, and AMPTP or IATSE contract materials.

Scenarios validate unsupported payroll or AP changes, missing production paperwork, cost-ledger mismatches, incentive-support exceptions, industry-overgeneralization, and unsupported override requests.

The package requires named production records such as deal memos, timecards, Exhibit G reports, payroll registers, petty-cash envelopes, invoices, DPRs, call sheets, and qualified-spend ledgers before the lane can act.
notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable retrieval of production accounting, payroll, AP, bank, and incentive-support records with low false-confidence escalation rates.
